---
title: "Evaluation: A Systematic Approach Practice Implications"
description: "Practice synthesis for Evaluation: A Systematic Approach."
book_id: "evaluation-systematic-approach"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Practice"
citation_key: "rossi_lipsey_freeman_2004_evaluation_systematic_approach"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:evaluation-systematic-approach:ch02-chapter-2-tailoring-evaluations:c0002"
  - "chunk:evaluation-systematic-approach:ch03-chapter-3-identifying-issues-and-formulating-questions:c0055"
  - "chunk:evaluation-systematic-approach:ch05-chapter-5-expressing-and-assessing-program-theory:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

This practice synthesis draws on selected notes from *Evaluation: A Systematic Approach* [@rossi_lipsey_freeman_2004_evaluation_systematic_approach].

## Evaluation Practice Implications

- Classify evaluation questions before selecting methods.
- Assess program theory and process before attempting impact claims.
- Use stakeholder relationships deliberately: independent, collaborative, or capacity-building.
- Prioritize questions that can influence understanding or decisions.

## Applying the Notes

Use these implications as prompts during scoping, design review, method selection, and reporting. They should be adapted to the evaluation's decision context, available evidence, and user needs.

## Links

- [Tailoring Evaluations](section-01-tailoring-evaluations.qmd)
- [Identifying Issues and Formulating Questions](section-02-formulating-questions.qmd)
- [Expressing and Assessing Program Theory](section-03-program-theory-assessment.qmd)

## References
